lint_frontend.details: 0 errors, 8 warnings (complexity and line-count warnings, pre-existing)

typecheck_backend.details: 95 errors in 13 files. Pre-existing technical debt documented in AGENTS.md (~119 known errors, now reduced to 95). Not a regression — actually an improvement.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance about suppressed findings and trust scores: 'Suppressed (false-positive) findings still count toward trust score calculations. If this behavior should change, explicitly document whether suppressed findings should be excluded from trust scores and executive summaries.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature regulatory-mapping-backend: audit_orchestrator.py:242 passes all finding_models (including suppressed) to _score_audit() and _build_summary(). Two separate reviewers flagged this as a design decision that needs documentation.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add to conventions: 'All taxonomy-related display data (regulation colors, names, descriptions, category labels) in frontend MUST be imported from src/constants/taxonomy.ts — never reconstructed inline via switch statements or local objects.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature advanced-intelligence-frontend: ReportPage.tsx:473-513 duplicates REGULATION_INFO via inline switch statement while FindingCard.tsx correctly imports from taxonomy.ts. This violates the single-source-of-truth convention.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add .gitignore entries for runtime artifacts: add 'server.log', 'server.err', 'server.pid', and 'node_modules/' (root-level) to .gitignore. Currently only frontend/node_modules/ is excluded.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature cross-area-integration-test: Commits 87a70a3, d89b1fc, 5f2b4fd all include server.log/server.err changes. Root node_modules/ directory was committed with vitest cache files. .gitignore only has 'frontend/node_modules/' not root 'node_modules/'.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify mypy standards: The 'Coding Conventions' section says 'Type hints everywhere (mypy strict mode)' while 'Known Pre-Existing Issues' acknowledges ~119 mypy errors. Workers are unclear whether to fix mypy errors or suppress with type: ignore. Suggest adding: 'Workers should not add new type: ignore comments. When encountering type errors in existing code, use typing.cast() or proper type annotations. Pre-existing type: ignore comments may remain.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature cross-area-integration-test: Commit 87a70a3 adds 5 new type: ignore comments in taxonomy.py and suppression.py. Multiple reviewers flagged the tension between 'mypy strict' convention and widespread type: ignore usage.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The frontend-dev skill should include a verification step to check that all className references in JSX have corresponding CSS definitions: 'Before committing, grep all custom className values and verify they have corresponding CSS rules in src/styles/index.css.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature advanced-intelligence-frontend: ReportPage.tsx uses 7 regulatory summary CSS classes (.regulatory-grid, .regulatory-card, etc.) that have no definitions in index.css. The section renders but without proper styling.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Cross-stack integration features should not be assigned the backend-dev skill exclusively. Consider creating a 'full-stack-dev' skill or requiring both backend-dev and frontend-dev skills when the feature description includes frontend verification requirements.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature cross-area-integration-test required 'Frontend: Verify history page shows audits, report page shows enhanced findings, PDF download works' but was assigned skillName 'backend-dev'. The worker only ran automated frontend tests/build but performed no interactive frontend verification.

rejectedObservations.observation: Worker commit message 'Fix test assertion for nova_ai_medium confidence threshold' is misleading for a commit with 4 new files and 1246 insertions
rejectedObservations.reason: This is about the second worker session for regulatory-mapping-backend, which was a re-run that included the first session's work. The commit message reflects the fix that made the second attempt succeed. Not actionable as a convention change — it's a consequence of worker session retries.

rejectedObservations.observation: Worker followed implementation-first approach rather than TDD as suggested by backend-dev skill
rejectedObservations.reason: Pragmatic deviation acknowledged. TDD is suggested, not required. The comprehensive test suite was delivered alongside implementation. Not a systemic issue.

rejectedObservations.observation: Frontend taxonomy.ts omits SCENARIO_RELEVANT_CATEGORIES from backend taxonomy.py
rejectedObservations.reason: The feature description says 'mirroring backend taxonomy' but this specific mapping isn't used by any current frontend feature. Adding unused constants would be unnecessary. Can be added when needed.
